summary: Kenneth demonstrates a weekly covered call strategy on HIMS, selling $25 premium contracts struck $1 out-of-the-money against 100-share positions. He explains the mechanics of assignment, profit scenarios, and why he doesn't roll contracts despite near-assignment situations, netting $300 this week versus $236 from outright stock trading.
takeaways: Sell covered calls $1 out-of-the-money on volatile weekly series stocks like HIMS
Target $25 premium per weekly contract (~5% return on $475 stock cost)
Win either way at assignment ($95 profit) or stock retention ($205 profit + premium)
Don't roll contracts when cost to buyback (~$400) exceeds profit from extension
Monitor stock price daily starting Thursday to anticipate assignment likelihood
Use for short-term income generation separate from long-term buy-and-hold positions
Stock volatility determines premium sustainability—switch stocks if premiums decline

strategy_candidates.change: Systematize weekly covered call selection by volatility threshold to consistently generate $25/week premium
strategy_candidates.evidence: Kenneth achieved $300 this week ($25 premium + $95 capital) on single HIMS contract; suggests replicable across multiple volatile tickers to scale Premium Desk toward $1,250/mo target

title: Part 4.5 - 6/27 $200 per week
